content:
# Revelation Chapter 11 — Disagreement Log
**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **Book:** Revelation | **Chapter:** 11

**Verse 1 — "saying" vs. "it was said" vs. "was told"; "reed like a rod" vs. "measuring rod like a staff":**
- EMET: "a reed like a rod, saying, 'Rise and measure the temple'"
- LOGOS: "a reed like a rod, and it was said, 'Rise and measure the temple'"
- PNEUMA: "a measuring rod like a staff and was told, 'Go and measure the temple...and count those who worship there'"
- *Resolution:* SOPHIA follows LOGOS. "It was said" resolves the grammatical issue of legōn modifying kalamos. PNEUMA reverses the comparison (kalamos homoios rhabdō = reed like a rod, not rod like a staff) and adds "count" for metrēson, which means "measure."

**Verse 2 — "cast out" vs. "exclude"; "was given" vs. "has been given" vs. "handed over":**
- EMET: "cast out...it was given to the nations"
- LOGOS: "cast it out...it has been given to the nations"
- PNEUMA: "exclude...it has been handed over to the nations"
- *Resolution:* SOPHIA follows LOGOS. "Cast it out" preserves ekbale. "Has been given" reflects the Greek passive. PNEUMA's "exclude" and "handed over" are interpretive.

**Verse 3 — "I shall give [authority]" vs. "I will give authority" vs. "I will empower"; number format:**
- EMET: "I shall give [authority]...a thousand two hundred sixty days"
- LOGOS: "I will give authority...one thousand two hundred and sixty days"
- PNEUMA: "I will empower...1,260 days"
- *Resolution:* SOPHIA follows LOGOS. "Empower" (PNEUMA) is interpretive. Numeral format (PNEUMA) breaks formal register.

**Verse 6 — "the heaven" vs. "heaven" vs. "the sky"; "wish" vs. "desire":**
- EMET: "shut the heaven...as often as they may wish"
- LOGOS: "shut heaven...as often as they desire"
- PNEUMA: "shut the sky...as often as they wish"
- *Resolution:* SOPHIA follows LOGOS. "Heaven" without article is standard; "sky" (PNEUMA) is too mundane. "Desire" adopted for thelōsin.

**Verse 7 — "finish" vs. "have finished"; "abyss" vs. "bottomless pit":**
- EMET: "when they finish their testimony...out of the abyss"
- LOGOS: "when they have finished their testimony...out of the abyss"
- PNEUMA: "When they have finished delivering their testimony...from the bottomless pit"
- *Resolution:* SOPHIA follows LOGOS. "Have finished" properly renders the aorist subjunctive. "Abyss" retained over "bottomless pit."

**Verse 8 — "corpse" (singular) vs. "dead bodies" (plural) vs. "bodies"; "spiritually" vs. "figuratively":**
- EMET: "their corpse" (singular, following Sinaiticus ptōma)
- LOGOS: "their dead bodies" (plural)
- PNEUMA: "Their bodies...figuratively called Sodom and Egypt"
- *Resolution:* SOPHIA renders "their dead body" (singular) honoring the Sinaiticus reading noted by EMET. "Spiritually" (EMET/LOGOS) retained over "figuratively" (PNEUMA) — pneumatikōs carries theological weight.

**Verse 9 — "see" vs. "look upon" vs. "stare at":**
- EMET: "see their corpse three days and a half"
- LOGOS: "look upon their dead bodies for three and a half days"
- PNEUMA: "stare at their bodies...refuse to let them be buried"
- *Resolution:* SOPHIA follows LOGOS. "Look upon" for blepousin. PNEUMA's "stare" and "refuse" are interpretive.

**Verse 10 — "dwelling upon" vs. "dwell upon" vs. "living on"; "make merry" vs. "celebrate":**
- EMET: "those dwelling upon the earth rejoice over them and make merry"
- LOGOS: "those who dwell upon the earth rejoice over them and make merry"
- PNEUMA: "Those living on the earth will celebrate over them and exchange gifts"
- *Resolution:* SOPHIA follows LOGOS. "Celebrate" and "exchange gifts" (PNEUMA) are dynamic paraphrases.

**Verse 11 — "[the] spirit" vs. "a spirit" vs. "the breath":**
- EMET: "[the] spirit of life from God"
- LOGOS: "a spirit of life from God"
- PNEUMA: "the breath of life from God"
- *Resolution:* SOPHIA follows LOGOS — anarthrous pneuma zōēs rendered "a spirit of life." "Breath" (PNEUMA) is interpretive.

**Verse 12 — "great voice" vs. "loud voice":**
- EMET: "a great voice out of the heaven"
- LOGOS: "a loud voice from heaven"
- PNEUMA: "a loud voice from heaven"
- *Resolution:* SOPHIA follows LOGOS/PNEUMA majority — "loud voice from heaven."

**Verse 13 — "names of men seven thousand" vs. "seven thousand people":**
- EMET: "names of men seven thousand" (follows Greek word order)
- LOGOS: "seven thousand names of men"
- PNEUMA: "Seven thousand people"
- *Resolution:* SOPHIA renders "seven thousand people" — the Hebraism onomata anthrōpōn means "persons/people," as PNEUMA correctly interprets.

**Verse 15 — "Christos" vs. "Christ"; "unto the ages of the ages" vs. "forever and ever":**
- EMET: "his Christos...unto the ages of the ages"
- LOGOS: "his Christ...forever and ever"
- PNEUMA: "his Christ...forever and ever"
- *Resolution:* SOPHIA follows LOGOS/PNEUMA — "Christ" and "forever and ever" are standard English renderings.

**Verse 17 — "Lord God the Almighty" vs. "Lord God Almighty":**
- EMET: "Lord God the Almighty, the [One] who is and the [One] who was"
- LOGOS: "Lord God Almighty, who is and who was"
- PNEUMA: "Lord God, the Almighty, the One who is and who was"
- *Resolution:* SOPHIA follows LOGOS — "Lord God Almighty" is the standard rendering.

**Verse 18 — "holy ones" vs. "saints" vs. "holy people"; "fearing" vs. "fear" vs. "honor":**
- EMET: "the holy ones...those fearing your name"
- LOGOS: "the saints...those who fear your name"
- PNEUMA: "your holy people...all who honor your name"
- *Resolution:* SOPHIA follows LOGOS — "saints" for hagiois, "fear" for phoboumenois. PNEUMA's "honor" softens the Greek.

**Verse 19 — "lightnings" vs. "flashes of lightning"; theophanic catalogue:**
- EMET: "lightnings and voices and thunders"
- LOGOS: "flashes of lightning and voices and thunders"
- PNEUMA: "flashes of lightning, rumblings, peals of thunder...a severe hailstorm"
- *Resolution:* SOPHIA follows LOGOS. PNEUMA's "rumblings," "peals of," and "severe hailstorm" embellish the Greek phōnai, brontai, and chalaza megalē.

---

*Signed, SOPHIA — in wisdom, seeking the faithful center where precision meets clarity*
